content.full_text:
Ann Batman
8 Alderwood Way
San Rafael, CA 94901
Re:
Your Request for Assistance
Our File No. G-97-355
Dear Ms. Batman:
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act was received on June 27,
1997, by the Fair Political Practices Commission.
The Political Reform Act (the “Act”)’ requires the Commission to provide formal written
advice to any person whose duties under the Act are in question or by that person’s authorized
representative. (Section 83114 (b).)
However, 2 Cal. Code Regulations Section 18329 (b) (8) (A) provides that written advice
will be declined where the “requestor is seeking advice relating to past conduct.” Consequently,
we are unable to provide written advice with respect to the issues you raise in your letter.
Nonetheless, we have provided you with a number of advice letters that may be of assistance to
you and a copy of Regulation 18329.
If you need assistance with respect to a specific prospective situation, you should request
further assistance. However, we will need specific facts regarding the situation to which the
request relates.
Ifyou have any further questions regarding this matter, please feel free to contact me at
(916) 322-5660.
DW:tls
Government Code Sections 81000-91015.
RAVE MEHTA
CH AIRMAN
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
August 8, 1997
Sincerely,
Graduate Assistant, Legal Division
428 J STREET, SUITE 800, SACRAMENTO Cit

sections.conclusion: Written advice will be declined where the requestor is seeking advice relating to past conduct, pursuant to 2 Cal. Code Regulations Section 18329(b)(8)(A). The FPPC is unable to provide written advice with respect to the issues raised in Ms. Batman's letter.
sections.question_synthetic: Ms. Batman sought FPPC advice regarding her duties under the Political Reform Act, but the specific subject matter of her inquiry related to past conduct.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declined to provide written advice because the request concerns past conduct, which is outside the scope of permissible advice letters under California Code of Regulations Section 18329(b)(8)(A). The FPPC encouraged Ms. Batman to resubmit if she has questions about prospective situations with specific facts.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a declination letter with no formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections. The legal basis for declination is clearly stated in the body of the letter. Request date was June 27, 1997; response date was August 8, 1997.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Ms. Batman sought FPPC advice regarding her duties under the Political Reform Act, but the specific subject matter of her inquiry related to past conduct.

CONCLUSION: Written advice will be declined where the requestor is seeking advice relating to past conduct, pursuant to 2 Cal. Code Regulations Section 18329(b)(8)(A). The FPPC is unable to provide written advice with respect to the issues raised in Ms. Batman's letter.

embedding.summary: The FPPC declined Ms. Batman's request for advice regarding her Political Reform Act duties because her inquiry related to past conduct rather than prospective situations. The FPPC provided reference materials and invited her to submit a new request if she has questions about future conduct.
